19     В‘ВҸВҮВӢВҶВҮВҗВ–ВӢ ВғВҺВ—ВҗВӢВ– В—В„ВҮВ•ВғВ”ВҮВ—В•ВҮВҶВ–В‘ В‘ВҗВ•В–В”В— В–ВғВ–ВҠВ”ВҮВҮЗҰВҶВӢВҸВҮВҗВ•ВӢВ‘ВҗВғВҺВ‘В„ВҢВҮ В–В„ВӣВүВҺВ—ВӢВҗВүВ–ВҠВҮВҸ
        В–В‘ВүВҮВ–ВҠВҮВ”ВҲВғ ВҮВ–В‘ВҲВғ ВҮЗӨ

        ВҚВҮВ– ВҠВҮВ•В‘ВҲВ–ВҠВӢВ•В‘В„ВҢВҮ В–ВғВ”ВҮВҸВғВҶВҮВ„ВӣВҺВ‘В‘ВҚВӢВҗВүВғВ–ВӢВ–ВҲВ”В‘ВҸВ–ВҠВҮВ”ВӢВүВҠВ–ЗҰВҠВғВҗВҶВ•ВӢВҶВҮЗЎВҲВ”В‘ВҸВ–ВҠВҮВҲВ”В‘ВҗВ–
        ВғВҗВҶВҲВ”В‘ВҸВғВ„В‘ВҳВҮЗӨВҠВҮВ•ВҮВ•ВҚВҮВ– ВҠВҮВ•ВғВ”ВҮ ВғВҺВҺВҮВҶВ–ВҠВҮВ•ВӢВҶВҮВҮВҺВҮВҳВғВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗЗЎВ–ВҠВҮВҲВ”В‘ВҗВ–ВҮВҺВҮВҳВғВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗЗЎВғВҗВҶВ–ВҠВҮ
        В’ВҺВғВҗВҳВӢВҮВҷВ”ВҮВ•В’ВҮ В–ВӢВҳВҮВҺВӣЗӨ



                                          ВҠВӢВ•ВӢВ•В–ВҠВҮВ•ВӢВҶВҮВҮВҺВҮВҳВғВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗВ‘ВҲВ–ВҠВҮВ‘В„ВҢВҮ В–ЗӨ

                                  

                                          ВҠВӢВ•ВӢВ•В–ВҠВҮВҲВ”В‘ВҗВ–ВҮВҺВҮВҳВғВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗВ‘ВҲВ–ВҠВҮВ‘В„ВҢВҮ В–ЗӨ

                                  

                                          ВҠВӢВ•ВӢВ•В–ВҠВҮВ’ВҺВғВҗВҳВӢВҮВҷВ‘ВҲВ–ВҠВҮВ‘В„ВҢВҮ В–ЗӨ

                                  
        

             В‘ВҷВҸВғВҗВӣ В—В„ВҮВ•ВҷВҮВ”ВҮВ—В•ВҮВҶВ–В‘ В‘ВҗВ•В–В”В— В–В–ВҠВҮВ‘В„ВҢВҮ В–З«

solution.rawText:
We first note that the object fits within a 3 Г— 3 cube. We begin with the plan view of the object:
all of the cubes must be within three layers fitting within this L-shape.
Let us show the three layers:

                                 ?                   ?                  ?

                                 ?                   ?                  ?

                       ?    ?    ?         ?    ?    ?         ?    ?   ?
                      bottom layer        middle layer          top layer

We have used вҖҳ?вҖҷ to indicate that we do not yet know where there are cubes. We do, however,
know that there must be a cube in at least one of the three layers for each of the five positions.
Next, let us look at the front elevation. This tells us that in the left hand column, we can see
two cubes. Since there is only one possible location for the cubes in this column, the first two
layers must have a cube and the third not. Likewise, for the middle column, there is only a cube
in the bottom layer. In the right hand column, there must be at least one cube in each layer,
but it is not yet clear where these would be, as there are three possible positions in each layer.
We now know the following:

                                 ?                   ?                  ?

                                 ?                   ?                  ?

                       Y    Y    ?         Y    x    ?         x   x    ?
                      bottom layer        middle layer          top layer

Next, let us consider the side elevation. The left hand column, which corresponds to the bottom
row of the plan view layers, has height 2, which means that the bottom right corner of the top
layer cannot have a cube. The middle column, which corresponds to the middle row of the plan
view layers, has height three, so all three of these cubes must be present (as there is only one
possible position in the middle row). Finally, the right column, corresponding to the top row of
the plan view layers, has height one, so only the bottom layer has a cube in this position.
We thus reach this position:

                                 Y                   x                  x

                                 Y                   Y                  Y

                       Y    Y    ?         Y    x    ?         x   x    x

                      bottom layer        middle layer          top layer

One of the вҖҳ?вҖҷ cubes must be present, to give the required plan view, and the other one may or
may not be вҖ“ it makes no difference to any of the views. So there are either 8 or 9 cubes, and
the answer is option F. (Note that we would need the corner of the bottom layer to be вҖҳYвҖҷ if it
is a single object.)
